metadata.title: The AI-capex depreciation & duration-mismatch trap: useful-life as the fifth self-marked number (Burry, Oracle FY2026, justdario)
metadata.status: primary claims FACT (Burry statements, Oracle 8-K, hyperscaler 10-K life changes); the unifying 'fifth self-marked number' framing is a labeled interpretation; formalized in models/z3/depreciation_trap.py
metadata.frame: Triggered by the justdario.com piece (2026-06) 'The Unhappy Ending of the Whole AI Dream'. Its checkable claims verify against primary data and add a mechanism the corpus had not formalized: AI compute depreciates faster (~2-3yr) than the debt/leases financing it (5-19yr), so even if revenue arrives the asset is gone before the debt is repaid - and the 'useful life' assumption that hides this is the SAME defect as the self-marked-value theorem (self_marked_value U1-U4), now applied to depreciation. Cross-refs fin-oracle / spec-sec-filings-primary, macro-bank-htm-marks, reflexive_marks.

article_assessed.thesis: the US AI datacenter buildout is debt-financed, fast-depreciating, winner-take-all capital waste with no graceful exit: abandoning triggers a Meta-metaverse-style stock crash, continuing requires perpetual capital, and (unlike Meta's cash-funded metaverse) the debt financing impairs the balance sheet and legacy operations when it unwinds; QE won't save it because debt service absorbs the cash flow.
article_assessed.alignment: strongly aligned with this project's machine-proven circular-core + insolvency-at-zero-inflow results; adds the depreciation/useful-life and asset-light->asset-heavy angles. status: the alignment is fact; the 'inevitable collapse' framing is the author's interpretation (this project proves STRUCTURE, not DATE - see spec-unwind-timing).

burry_depreciation.claim: hyperscalers (Meta/Amazon/MSFT/Google/Oracle) depreciate Nvidia GPUs over 5-6yr when true economic life is ~2-3yr; ~$176B understated depreciation / overstated profit 2026-2028; ~$50-60B/yr if true life is 3 not 6.
burry_depreciation.examples: Meta raised most server/network life to 5.5yr, cutting depreciation ~$2.3B over 9 months of 2025; Microsoft's ~$17B GPU purchases booked over 6yr vs 3yr -> ~$2.9B/yr earnings overstatement.
burry_depreciation.status: fact (Burry's statements, Nov 2025) + contested (the $176B is his estimate; 'probably not fraud but optimistic estimates that will require adjustment'). The useful-life IS a discretionary assumption - that part is fact.

oracle_asset_heavy_pivot.claim: Oracle shifted from asset-light to asset-heavy/debt-financed: FY2026 free cash flow NEGATIVE ~$23.7B, ~$50B capex, >$108B debt (raised +$30B IG bonds + mandatory convertible preferred early 2026).
oracle_asset_heavy_pivot.backlog_concentration: $523B RPO with >half resting on ONE customer (OpenAI via the ~$300B Stargate deal); $75B of the large AI contracts are customer-prepaid or customer-supplied GPUs (reduces Oracle's own raise).
oracle_asset_heavy_pivot.off_balance_sheet: $248B of additional datacenter/cloud LEASE commitments of 15-19yr term, substantially all not yet on the balance sheet - long-tenor financing against ~2-3yr-life GPUs ('Oracle is building yesterday's data centers with tomorrow's debt').

geopolitical_color.china: China ~$295bn state-directed AI buildout requiring 80%+ domestically produced semiconductors (per article; ~ one year of Google's spend). status: contested (single-source figure here; directionally consistent with China's self-sufficiency drive).
geopolitical_color.us_concentration: US operational datacenters reportedly exceed all other countries combined / ~10x Germany. status: weak (magnitude unverified here).

unified_useful_life_table_2026.summary: Per-company audit of disclosed server/GPU useful life + earnings effect, and the unified forward rate (#54). Disclosed lives are fact; economic-life and Burry overstatement figures are contested.

by_company.effect: ~$3.9B FY23 lower depreciation / ~$3.0B higher net income
shifting to 4yr adds ~$315M/qtr -> flips to a loss

unified_useful_life_table_2026.amazon_reversal: Amazon is the ONLY hyperscaler to publicly CUT a useful-life category (6->5yr, Jan 2025), citing 'an increased pace of technology development, particularly in artificial intelligence and machine learning' - an inside-the-filings admission the extend-life trend is reversing.
unified_useful_life_table_2026.coreweave_vs_nebius: Identical neocloud models, GPUs over 6yr (CoreWeave) vs 4yr (Nebius) - a ~50% depreciation-rate gap from the useful-life CHOICE alone; at 4yr CoreWeave's reported operating profit flips negative.
unified_useful_life_table_2026.burry_critique: Scion (Nov 2025): ~$176B understated depreciation 2026-28; Oracle ~26.9%, Meta ~20.8% overstated by 2028; ~$1.1B Nvidia+Palantir puts (later wound down). Nvidia rebuttal (Kress: 6-yr-old A100s 'still at full utilization') vs Burry (utilization != value; older chips far less power-efficient). All changes disclosed prospectively - 'fraud' is rhetorical/contested.
unified_useful_life_table_2026.unified_forward_rate: Cluster ~6yr accounting life vs a defensible ~3-4yr blended GPU economic life (training-edge ~1.5-2yr; inference tail ~3-4yr) vs Nvidia's ~annual cadence (Hopper->Blackwell->Rubin). Marking GPU-heavy fleets from ~6yr to ~3-4yr raises annual depreciation ~50-100% on the affected base. Key tension: accounting life is set on BLENDED fleets (incl. 15+yr shell/power); GPUs are the fast-decaying subset the blended life masks (the D2/D4 mechanism).
unified_useful_life_table_2026.grade: disclosed lives + dollar guides FACT; economic-life + Burry overstatement CONTESTED; not legally adjudicated.

the_mechanism_formalized.duration_mismatch: If asset economic life La (~3yr GPUs) < financing tenor Ld (5-7yr bonds, 15-19yr leases), there is an interval (La, Ld) where the asset book value is zero but the debt is still outstanding - a residual liability with no asset. Even if the planned revenue materializes, the thing it was borrowed to buy is gone before the loan is repaid.
the_mechanism_formalized.depreciation_is_timing: Total depreciation over an asset's life is fixed (=capex); a longer assumed life only DEFERS expense and inflates current profit, leaving an un-depreciated residual that must be written off at retirement (a catch-up). No life choice avoids both near-term losses and the eventual writedown.
the_mechanism_formalized.fifth_self_marked_number: Useful life joins HTM cost, AI fair-value marks, private-credit NAVs, and insurance captive marks as a CHOSEN number held above realizable until a forcing event - here the event is asset RETIREMENT/obsolescence. Same defect, fifth locus.
the_mechanism_formalized.z3_model: models/z3/depreciation_trap.py - D1 SAT (long life prints a profit), D2 UNSAT (honest short life strictly lowers profit; ~$2.9B/yr MSFT-like), D3 UNSAT (equity can't stay whole when La<Ld), D4 UNSAT (no schedule avoids both losses and a writedown).

sources.name: justdario.com - The Unhappy Ending of the Whole AI Dream (2026-06)
Dave Friedman - The $176 Billion Accounting Question at the Heart of the AI Boom (Burry)
The Motley Fool - Michael Burry just sent a warning to AI stocks
National Law Review / Deep Quarry - useful lives of GPUs, key considerations + earnings risk
Oracle Corp - FY2026 Form 8-K (Q4 results, FCF/capex/debt)
CNBC - Oracle is building yesterday's data centers with tomorrow's debt
